articles.headline: India Unveils 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Vision 2047' to Become Global Hub
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the comprehensive 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Vision 2047', outlining India's strategy to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
• The vision targets achieving 25 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2047, alongside significant cost reduction and development of indigenous electrolyser manufacturing capabilities.
• Emphasis is placed on creating a robust ecosystem for R&D, skill development, and establishing green hydrogen corridors across industrial clusters.
• This initiative is poised to significantly reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, enhance energy security, and contribute substantially to global decarbonization efforts.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen, and viability gap funding for pilot projects, are central to the vision.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Landmark Ruling on Digital Privacy and AI Ethics
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment reaffirming the right to privacy in the digital age, particularly in the context of rapidly evolving Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies.
• The ruling mandates stricter regulations for data collection, processing, and algorithmic decision-making by both state and private entities, emphasizing informed consent and data minimization.
• The Court highlighted the need for a robust legal framework to address algorithmic bias and ensure transparency and accountability in AI applications affecting citizens' rights.
• It urged the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that specifically incorporates principles of AI ethics and responsible innovation.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government surveillance programs, and the future of AI development in India.

articles.headline: India-EU Strategic Partnership Review Concludes; Focus on Trade, Climate, and Tech Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-European Union Strategic Partnership Review meeting concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to deepen cooperation across various sectors.
• Key discussions revolved around accelerating negotiations for the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA), with both sides expressing optimism for an early conclusion.
• Climate action and green transition initiatives were central, including joint projects on renewable energy, circular economy, and sustainable development goals.
• Enhanced collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, digital governance, and resilient supply chains also featured prominently.
• The dialogue underscored the shared values of democracy, human rights, and multilateralism, aiming to strengthen the rules-based international order.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Signals Cautious Optimism Amid Global Headwinds; Inflation Outlook Stable
articles.explainer:
• In a year-end press briefing, the RBI Governor expressed cautious optimism about India's economic trajectory despite persistent global headwinds like geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions.
• The Governor indicated that while inflation remains within the target band, vigilance is crucial, and future monetary policy decisions will be data-dependent.
• Emphasis was laid on maintaining financial stability and supporting sustainable growth, with the RBI ready to deploy calibrated measures as needed.
• The central bank also highlighted the resilience of India's banking sector and the ongoing efforts to strengthen regulatory frameworks for non-banking financial companies (NBFCs).
• Projections for Q4 FY2025 GDP growth were shared, indicating a steady but moderate expansion, driven by domestic demand and capital expenditure.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, demonstrating critical technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control necessary for future orbital re-entry and landing missions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing the cost of space access.
• The RLV program is central to India's long-term space exploration goals, including future human spaceflight missions and commercial satellite launches.
• Scientists lauded the indigenous development of advanced materials and avionics systems used in the RLV prototype, showcasing India's growing self-reliance in space technology.

articles.headline: New National Policy on Wetland Conservation and Management Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive National Policy on Wetland Conservation and Management, aiming to protect and restore India's vital wetland ecosystems.
• The policy emphasizes a participatory approach, involving local communities in wetland management plans and promoting sustainable livelihood options.
• It mandates mapping and categorization of all wetlands across the country, with specific guidelines for their ecological restoration, pollution control, and biodiversity preservation.
• The policy also includes provisions for strengthened legal frameworks and enforcement mechanisms to prevent encroachment and degradation of wetlands.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing climate change resilience, maintaining biodiversity, and ensuring water security for various regions.

articles.headline: Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0 Draft Released for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released the draft of the 'Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0' for public consultation.
• The new framework aims to strengthen India's cyber defenses against evolving threats, focusing on critical infrastructure protection, data governance, and cyber-crime prevention.
• It proposes enhanced collaboration between government agencies, private sector, and international partners to build a resilient cyber ecosystem.
• Key provisions include mandatory security audits for critical information infrastructure, establishment of a National Cyber Security Research & Development Fund, and capacity building programs.
• This update reflects the growing sophistication of cyber threats and the need for a proactive, multi-stakeholder approach to national cyber security.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Bill to Enhance Judicial Efficiency and Reduce Pendency
articles.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the 'Judicial Efficiency and Timely Justice Bill, 2025', aimed at significantly reducing the backlog of cases across Indian courts.
• The Bill introduces several reforms, including the establishment of specialized fast-track courts for specific categories of cases, and increased use of alternative dispute resolution (ADR) mechanisms.
• It mandates the adoption of advanced digital infrastructure for case management, e-filing, and virtual hearings to streamline judicial processes.
• Provisions for increasing the number of judges in lower courts and enhancing judicial training programs are also included to bolster the judicial workforce.
• Legal experts anticipate that the Bill, once enacted, will play a crucial role in improving access to justice and upholding the rule of law in India.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Make in India for the World' Export Promotion Scheme
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the 'Make in India for the World' scheme, a new export promotion initiative designed to boost domestic manufacturing and integrate India into global supply chains.
• The scheme provides targeted incentives, financial support, and infrastructure upgrades for industries with high export potential, focusing on sectors like electronics, textiles, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components.
• It aims to enhance India's competitiveness in international markets by improving product quality, reducing logistics costs, and facilitating market access.
• The initiative also includes provisions for skill development and technological upgradation to meet global manufacturing standards.
• This scheme is a strategic move to leverage India's manufacturing capabilities for global markets, aligning with the broader vision of an 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global South Development at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to strengthening cooperation with the Global South, announcing new initiatives and enhanced financial support at a special UN forum on South-South Cooperation.
• The initiatives focus on capacity building, technology transfer, and infrastructure development projects in partner countries, particularly in Africa and Small Island Developing States (SIDS).
• India emphasized its approach of 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (The World is One Family) and its role as a responsible and reliable development partner.
• Discussions included collaborative efforts on climate resilience, food security, digital public infrastructure, and access to affordable healthcare.
• This pledge reinforces India's growing influence as a voice for developing nations on the international stage and its commitment to multilateralism.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of telemedicine services under the National Health Mission (NHM), targeting remote and underserved rural areas.
• This initiative aims to bridge the gap in healthcare access by leveraging digital technology to connect patients in rural clinics with specialist doctors in urban centers.
• New mobile health units equipped with teleconsultation facilities and diagnostic tools will be deployed, along with training programs for local health workers.
• The expansion focuses on improving maternal and child health outcomes, managing non-communicable diseases, and providing mental health support.
• This move is a crucial step towards achieving universal health coverage and strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure, especially post-pandemic.

articles.headline: Major Cities Launch 'Clean Air Challenge' Initiative with Tech Integration
articles.explainer:
• A consortium of India's major metropolitan cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru, jointly launched the 'Clean Air Challenge' initiative.
• The initiative aims for a significant reduction in air pollution levels by 2030 through a multi-pronged strategy involving advanced monitoring, data analytics, and citizen participation.
• It integrates smart city technologies, AI-powered predictive models for pollution hotspots, and real-time public dashboards to enhance transparency and accountability.
• Key measures include promotion of electric vehicles, strict enforcement of industrial emission norms, waste management reforms, and green infrastructure development.
• The 'Clean Air Challenge' seeks to foster inter-city learning and collaboration to tackle the persistent problem of urban air quality.

• The government pledged enhanced support for further research, development, and commercialization of this technology, inviting private sector partnerships.
• Experts believe this breakthrough could position India as a key player in the global semiconductor ecosystem within the next decade.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Completes Review for Union Territories
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission announced the completion of its exercise for redrawing parliamentary and assembly constituencies in several Union Territories (UTs).
• The review aimed to ensure fair representation based on recent census data and geographical contiguity, while adhering to constitutional provisions.
• The Commission's recommendations include adjustments in the number of seats, reservation for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs), and boundary realignments.
• Stakeholder consultations were conducted extensively with political parties, public representatives, and civil society organizations before finalizing the report.
• This exercise is a crucial step for ensuring equitable electoral representation and democratic processes in the concerned Union Territories.

articles.headline: Infrastructure Push: New High-Speed Rail Corridor Project Approved

• The project, estimated at ₹1.5 lakh crore, aims to significantly reduce travel time, boost regional connectivity, and stimulate economic development along the corridor.
• It will incorporate advanced 'Make in India' components and technologies, promoting indigenous manufacturing and job creation.
• The corridor is designed with a focus on environmental sustainability, including measures for minimal land acquisition impact and energy-efficient operations.
• This approval underscores the government's continued commitment to modernizing India's infrastructure and enhancing multimodal logistics capabilities.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Dialogue on Connectivity and Energy Security Concludes
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Central Asia Dialogue concluded, with participating nations reaffirming their commitment to enhancing regional connectivity and cooperation on energy security.
• Key discussions focused on operationalizing the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) and exploring new transit routes to boost trade and economic linkages.
• Energy cooperation included proposals for joint ventures in renewable energy projects and mechanisms for stable supply of hydrocarbons to India.
• Both sides emphasized combating terrorism, extremism, and drug trafficking, agreeing on greater intelligence sharing and coordinated security efforts.
• The dialogue underscored India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its strategic importance for regional stability and prosperity.

articles.headline: Ganga Rejuvenation Mission Enters Phase III, Focus on Tributaries and Public Participation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase III of the Ganga Rejuvenation Mission, shifting focus to rejuvenating tributaries and enhancing public participation.
• This phase prioritizes the comprehensive treatment of wastewater from major towns along the tributaries, addressing non-point source pollution and industrial discharges.
• New initiatives include community-led river conservation programs, promotion of organic farming in riverine areas, and ecological restoration of floodplains.
• Advanced technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and bioremediation techniques will be deployed more extensively.
• Phase III aims to build upon the successes of previous phases, ensuring a holistic approach to restore the ecological health and cultural significance of the entire Ganga basin.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Vision 2047', consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve 25 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2047.
2. It primarily focuses on imports of green hydrogen to meet India's energy needs.
3. The vision includes Production-Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The vision targets achieving 25 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2047. Statement 2 is incorrect. The vision emphasizes indigenous production and export potential, aiming to reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports, not green hydrogen imports. Statement 3 is correct. Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen, are central to the vision.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary focus area of the recently released draft 'Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0'?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0 primarily focuses on strengthening cyber defenses against evolving threats, including critical infrastructure protection, data governance, and cyber-crime prevention. While social media content might have indirect security implications, its direct 'regulation' is not listed as a primary focus area of this specific cybersecurity framework.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program:
1. The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment.
2. The program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access.
3. It focuses exclusively on commercial satellite launches, excluding human spaceflight missions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The test involved a controlled landing experiment, demonstrating critical technologies for autonomous navigation. Statement 2 is correct. The RLV program aims at drastically reducing the cost of space access. Statement 3 is incorrect. The RLV program is central to India's long-term space exploration goals, including future human spaceflight missions and commercial satellite launches.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Judicial Efficiency and Timely Justice Bill, 2025' passed by Parliament aims to address judicial pendency through which of the following measures?
1. Establishment of specialized fast-track courts.
2. Increased use of Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) mechanisms.
3. Mandating digital infrastructure for case management.
4. Providing for direct appointment of judges by the executive without judicial consultation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct measures included in the Bill to enhance judicial efficiency and reduce pendency. Statement 4 is incorrect. Such a provision would likely violate the principle of judicial independence and the basic structure of the Constitution, and is not a typical reform measure aimed at efficiency but rather a fundamental change in judicial appointment. The Bill typically focuses on procedural and infrastructural improvements, not altering the fundamental appointment process to bypass judicial consultation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following describes the 'G20 Troika'?

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Issues Landmark Ruling on Digital Privacy and AI Ethics
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment reaffirming the right to privacy in the digital age, particularly in the context of rapidly evolving Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies.
• The ruling mandates stricter regulations for data collection, processing, and algorithmic decision-making by both state and private entities, emphasizing informed consent and data minimization.
• The Court highlighted the need for a robust legal framework to address algorithmic bias and ensure transparency and accountability in AI applications affecting citizens' rights.
• It urged the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that specifically incorporates principles of AI ethics and responsible innovation.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government surveillance programs, and the future of AI development in India.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Bill to Enhance Judicial Efficiency and Reduce Pendency
GS2.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the 'Judicial Efficiency and Timely Justice Bill, 2025', aimed at significantly reducing the backlog of cases across Indian courts.
• The Bill introduces several reforms, including the establishment of specialized fast-track courts for specific categories of cases, and increased use of alternative dispute resolution (ADR) mechanisms.
• It mandates the adoption of advanced digital infrastructure for case management, e-filing, and virtual hearings to streamline judicial processes.
• Provisions for increasing the number of judges in lower courts and enhancing judicial training programs are also included to bolster the judicial workforce.
• Legal experts anticipate that the Bill, once enacted, will play a crucial role in improving access to justice and upholding the rule of law in India.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Completes Review for Union Territories

• The Commission's recommendations include adjustments in the number of seats, reservation for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs), and boundary realignments.
• Stakeholder consultations were conducted extensively with political parties, public representatives, and civil society organizations before finalizing the report.
• This exercise is a crucial step for ensuring equitable electoral representation and democratic processes in the concerned Union Territories.

• The Union Cabinet approved the comprehensive 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Vision 2047', outlining India's strategy to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
• The vision targets achieving 25 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity by 2047, alongside significant cost reduction and development of indigenous electrolyser manufacturing capabilities.
• Emphasis is placed on creating a robust ecosystem for R&D, skill development, and establishing green hydrogen corridors across industrial clusters.
• This initiative is poised to significantly reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, enhance energy security, and contribute substantially to global decarbonization efforts.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolysers and green hydrogen, and viability gap funding for pilot projects, are central to the vision.

GS3.headline: RBI Governor Signals Cautious Optimism Amid Global Headwinds; Inflation Outlook Stable
GS3.explainer:
• In a year-end press briefing, the RBI Governor expressed cautious optimism about India's economic trajectory despite persistent global headwinds like geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions.
• The Governor indicated that while inflation remains within the target band, vigilance is crucial, and future monetary policy decisions will be data-dependent.
• Emphasis was laid on maintaining financial stability and supporting sustainable growth, with the RBI ready to deploy calibrated measures as needed.
• The central bank also highlighted the resilience of India's banking sector and the ongoing efforts to strengthen regulatory frameworks for non-banking financial companies (NBFCs).
• Projections for Q4 FY2025 GDP growth were shared, indicating a steady but moderate expansion, driven by domestic demand and capital expenditure.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, demonstrating critical technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control necessary for future orbital re-entry and landing missions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing the cost of space access.
• The RLV program is central to India's long-term space exploration goals, including future human spaceflight missions and commercial satellite launches.
• Scientists lauded the indigenous development of advanced materials and avionics systems used in the RLV prototype, showcasing India's growing self-reliance in space technology.

GS3.headline: New National Policy on Wetland Conservation and Management Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive National Policy on Wetland Conservation and Management, aiming to protect and restore India's vital wetland ecosystems.
• The policy emphasizes a participatory approach, involving local communities in wetland management plans and promoting sustainable livelihood options.
• It mandates mapping and categorization of all wetlands across the country, with specific guidelines for their ecological restoration, pollution control, and biodiversity preservation.
• The policy also includes provisions for strengthened legal frameworks and enforcement mechanisms to prevent encroachment and degradation of wetlands.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing climate change resilience, maintaining biodiversity, and ensuring water security for various regions.

GS3.headline: Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0 Draft Released for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released the draft of the 'Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework 2.0' for public consultation.
• The new framework aims to strengthen India's cyber defenses against evolving threats, focusing on critical infrastructure protection, data governance, and cyber-crime prevention.
• It proposes enhanced collaboration between government agencies, private sector, and international partners to build a resilient cyber ecosystem.
• Key provisions include mandatory security audits for critical information infrastructure, establishment of a National Cyber Security Research & Development Fund, and capacity building programs.
• This update reflects the growing sophistication of cyber threats and the need for a proactive, multi-stakeholder approach to national cyber security.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Make in India for the World' Export Promotion Scheme
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the 'Make in India for the World' scheme, a new export promotion initiative designed to boost domestic manufacturing and integrate India into global supply chains.
• The scheme provides targeted incentives, financial support, and infrastructure upgrades for industries with high export potential, focusing on sectors like electronics, textiles, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components.
• It aims to enhance India's competitiveness in international markets by improving product quality, reducing logistics costs, and facilitating market access.
• The initiative also includes provisions for skill development and technological upgradation to meet global manufacturing standards.
• This scheme is a strategic move to leverage India's manufacturing capabilities for global markets, aligning with the broader vision of an 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.

GS3.headline: Major Cities Launch 'Clean Air Challenge' Initiative with Tech Integration
GS3.explainer:
• A consortium of India's major metropolitan cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru, jointly launched the 'Clean Air Challenge' initiative.
• The initiative aims for a significant reduction in air pollution levels by 2030 through a multi-pronged strategy involving advanced monitoring, data analytics, and citizen participation.
• It integrates smart city technologies, AI-powered predictive models for pollution hotspots, and real-time public dashboards to enhance transparency and accountability.
• Key measures include promotion of electric vehicles, strict enforcement of industrial emission norms, waste management reforms, and green infrastructure development.
• The 'Clean Air Challenge' seeks to foster inter-city learning and collaboration to tackle the persistent problem of urban air quality.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Semiconductor Manufacturing Process Announced
GS3.explainer:
• Indian researchers and engineers announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous semiconductor manufacturing process, marking a critical step towards self-reliance in chip production.
• The innovation involves a novel fabrication technique that reduces production costs and energy consumption, making it competitive with global standards.
• This advancement is crucial for India's strategic autonomy in electronics, defense, and telecommunications, reducing dependence on foreign supply chains.
• The government pledged enhanced support for further research, development, and commercialization of this technology, inviting private sector partnerships.
• Experts believe this breakthrough could position India as a key player in the global semiconductor ecosystem within the next decade.

GS3.headline: Infrastructure Push: New High-Speed Rail Corridor Project Approved
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet gave its approval for a new high-speed rail (HSR) corridor project, connecting two major economic hubs in the country, as part of the National Rail Plan 2030.
• The project, estimated at ₹1.5 lakh crore, aims to significantly reduce travel time, boost regional connectivity, and stimulate economic development along the corridor.
• It will incorporate advanced 'Make in India' components and technologies, promoting indigenous manufacturing and job creation.
• The corridor is designed with a focus on environmental sustainability, including measures for minimal land acquisition impact and energy-efficient operations.
• This approval underscores the government's continued commitment to modernizing India's infrastructure and enhancing multimodal logistics capabilities.

GS3.headline: Ganga Rejuvenation Mission Enters Phase III, Focus on Tributaries and Public Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase III of the Ganga Rejuvenation Mission, shifting focus to rejuvenating tributaries and enhancing public participation.
• This phase prioritizes the comprehensive treatment of wastewater from major towns along the tributaries, addressing non-point source pollution and industrial discharges.
• New initiatives include community-led river conservation programs, promotion of organic farming in riverine areas, and ecological restoration of floodplains.
• Advanced technologies for real-time water quality monitoring and bioremediation techniques will be deployed more extensively.
• Phase III aims to build upon the successes of previous phases, ensuring a holistic approach to restore the ecological health and cultural significance of the entire Ganga basin.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Matsya Sampada Plus' for Sustainable Fisheries Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying launched 'Matsya Sampada Plus', an enhanced scheme building on the success of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY).
• This new scheme focuses on sustainable intensification of aquaculture, deep-sea fishing expansion, and improving post-harvest infrastructure and market linkages.
• It introduces advanced technologies for disease management in aquaculture, promotes climate-resilient fishing practices, and provides specialized training for fisherfolk.
• The 'Matsya Sampada Plus' aims to further boost fish production, enhance incomes of fisher communities, and ensure food security while conserving aquatic biodiversity.
• Financial outlay and targets for the scheme over the next five years were also announced.
